business.headline: Sensex, Nifty slip as rupee hits record lowThe benchmark BSE Sensex fell 160.73 points, or 0.21%, to close at 75,237.99, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 46.10 points, or 0.19%, to settle at 23,643.50.

Reliance-Disney's JioStar sues Zee over alleged unauthorised film broadcasts: ReportJioStar has moved against Zee over alleged unauthorised telecasts of 12 Bollywood films. The clash adds to a widening rights battle between two major media companies.

Maharashtra cuts VAT on jet fuel from 18% to 7% amid rising aviation costsFuel is one of the biggest expenses for airlines and accounts for nearly 35-40% of an airline's operating costs. The tax reduction is expected to lower refuelling costs at Mumbai airport, which is India's second-busiest airport after Delhi and handles around 15% of the country's air traffic.

Petrol, diesel prices could rise over next 3-4 months amid crude oil crisisThe Rs 3-per-litre fuel price hike may not be the last, with analysts warning that persistently high crude oil prices could trigger more petrol and diesel price increases over coming three to four months.
Rupee hits all-time low, breaches 96 for 1st time against US dollarThe rupee weakened 0.3% to 96.05 against the dollar, crossing its previous all-time low of 95.9575 touched in the previous session.

HPCL dealers allege pressure to push 'Power Petrol' amid fuel price hikePetrol dealers have alleged that state-run oil firms are pressuring them to prioritise premium fuel orders amid a fresh Rs 3 per litre hike. The claim has added to concerns over consumer choice and retail fuel availability during the West Asia-linked energy crisis.

Beyond the petrol pump: How higher fuel prices could hit India's economyThe Rs 3-per-litre petrol and diesel price hike may only be the start of a new cycle of fuel price increases as crude oil prices remain high, with economists warning of wider pressure on inflation, growth, household budgets and the rupee

Petrol, diesel prices hiked after years. Here's what else could get costlierRising petrol and diesel prices are starting to push up transport and household costs across India. Economists say the impact could spread to food, deliveries, air travel and inflation if crude stays high.
Sensex, Nifty open higher despite rise in fuel prices; IT stocks lead gainsThe S&P BSE Sensex was up 292.55 points to 75,691.27, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 83.70 points to 23,773.30 as of 9:34 am.

Fuel costlier by Rs 3 per litre: Why government raised petrol, diesel pricesThe government and state-run oil companies raised petrol and diesel prices by Rs 3 per litre on Friday after keeping rates unchanged for weeks.

8th Pay Commission: What happened at the Cabinet Secretary-led NCJCM meetingAt the NCJCM meeting on May 11, employee unions placed pay, pension, healthcare and staffing demands before senior government officials. The discussion gained added weight as it ran alongside 8th Pay Commission consultations and prompted directions on several pending issues.

India's fuel crisis could push a long-overdue rethink of how cities movePM Modi's seven appeals are the right signal at the right time. But fuel security and clean air require infrastructure, not just intention, and that demands a place in every state and central budget.

Petrol, diesel prices hiked by Rs 3 nationwide; CNG also up in Delhi by Rs 2  Live UpdatesIndia had so far avoided a major increase in retail fuel prices despite surging global crude oil prices as a result of the West Asian conflict. However, speculation over another round of hikes had been building for days as global crude continued to climb.

Govt caps duty-free gold imports at 100 kg under Advance Authorisation schemeGold imports in India, the world's second-biggest gold consumer after China, are driven by the jewellery industry demand. Such imports involve a substantial outflow of foreign exchange.

US moving to end $265 million fraud cases against Gautam Adani: ReportThe US Justice Department is reportedly set to drop criminal fraud and bribery charges against Gautam Adani linked to an alleged $265 million corruption scheme tied to India's largest solar power project, according to a Bloomberg report.

We asked top banks how they fight digital fraud. None respondedIn the third and final part of the series, Banking Risks in Digital India, IndiaToday.in reached out to major banks to understand how they are preparing for the next generation of digital fraud — from deepfakes and mule accounts to coercion-driven scams and AI-enabled deception. None of the banks responded.

Air India reports $2.8 billion loss in FY26, biggest since Tata takeoverAccording to Singapore Airlines' annual report, Air India's losses stood at 3.56 billion Singapore dollars during the financial year.

Paytm's pivotal year: How Vijay Shekhar Sharma steered Indian fintech's boldest turnaround storyPaytm reported its first full-year profit of Rs 552 crore in FY26, capping a sharp reset under Vijay Shekhar Sharma. The numbers shift attention from last year's regulatory shock to cost discipline, UPI growth and an AI-led strategy.

Your office commute is getting costlier. Is 'work from home' the answer?Getting to office is starting to feel expensive. With fuel price hike concerns growing and salaries already stretched by inflation and EMIs, employees are increasingly asking whether work from home deserves a second look.

China shows interest in US oil after Trump-Xi talks on Iran and Strait of HormuzAccording to a White House readout, Trump and Xi agreed that the Strait of Hormuz should be reopened to support global energy needs. The waterway has effectively remained closed since the start of the US and Israel conflict with Iran.

entertainment.headline: Matt Reeves confirms Scarlett Johansson, Sebastian Stan in The Batman: Part II Matt Reeves has unveiled a new cast for The Batman Part II. Along with Robert Pattinson in the title role, the film will star Scarlett Johansson, Sebastian Stan and Charles Dance. The new adventure set in Gotham City promises to be colder, as it tells a story of corruption and family scars.

Fashion first, cinema later: How Cannes became India's favourite runway From couture chaos to celebrity frenzy, Cannes 2026 has reignited an old debate in India. Is the world's most prestigious film festival still about cinema, or has the red carpet stolen the spotlight completely?

JioStar takes legal action against Zee over unauthorised telecast of Bollywood films In a 120-page plea, JioStar alleged that Zee Entertainment Enterprises broadcast 12 distinct films nearly 20 times, including several blockbusters starring Shah Rukh Khan and Aamir Khan.

Zakir Khan meets OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, calls it 'desi pull in Silicon Valley' Zakir Khan met OpenAI chief Sam Altman at his San Francisco home and shared photos from the visit. The unexpected crossover quickly drew online attention, even as Khan remains away from regular touring.

Karuppu review: A nostalgic but uneven ride with Suriya at his bestRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Karuppu movie review: Director RJ Balaji's Karuppu, starring Suriya, Trisha and himself, is a fantasy courtroom drama that exposes how corruption is deeply ingrained in the system. While the film taps into a familiar idea, it doesn't capitalise and use it to its full potential.

Kamal Haasan cheers 'brother' Suriya after Karuppu release Kamal Haasan congratulated Suriya, RJ Balaji and the Karuppu team after the film finally reached theatres today. The film, which was scheduled to hit theatres on May 14, also stars Trisha Krishnan.

Dhurandhar 2 on OTT: Ranveer's film to premiere in June with a twist. Check date Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is set to premiere on JioHotstar on June 4 with a 30-minute pre-show from 7 pm. The platform says viewers will see the film in its original and unfiltered form before wider subscriber access begins on June 5.

Welcome To The Jungle teaser: Akshay Kumar's fake film leads to total madness The teaser for Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle introduces a fake film plot that spirals into jungle chaos, scaling up the franchise's slapstick roots for a large-scale adventure.

Cannes 2026: Manisha Makwana on screening of debut film Gudgudi, Raj & DK Manisha Makwana's debut short Gudgudi will screen at Cannes 2026 in the Marche du Film segment. The screening marks a major step for the first-time director, who says Raj and DK and Suman Kumar shaped the film.

Pati Patni Aur Woh Do review: Funny, harmless and comfortably paisa vasoolRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Pati Patni Aur Woh Do brings Ayushmann Khurrana back to his small-town comic comfort zone. The film stays light and crowd-pleasing and avoids saying anything deeper - a clean, breezy entertainer.

Stuart Fails to Save the Universe brings multiverse chaos in first teaser HBO Max unveiled the first teaser for Stuart Fails to Save the Universe and confirmed its release date. The new Big Bang Theory spin-off promises multiverse chaos and familiar faces.

Suriya's Karuppu show turns festive as Trisha, Karthi join fans at Chennai theatre Karuppu finally opened in Chennai on Friday. To mark the occasion, director RJ Balaji, Trisha Krishnan, Sai Abhyankkar and Karthi joined Suriya fans at Rohini Theatre.

Pati Patni Aur Woh Do X review: Ayushmann's quirky comedy gets a mixed verdict Ayushmann Khurrana's Pati Patni Aur Woh Do was praised for its comic and quirky moments. Sara Ali Khan and Rakul Preet Singh received accolades amid mixed reviews from viewers.

Kartavya review: Saif Ali Khan elevates a caste-crime thriller that plays too safeRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Actor Saif Ali Khan's Kartavya turns a small-town murder probe into a study of caste, crime and duty. The film's ideas land, but its soft-edged execution blunts the force of its anger.

Sonu Sood slams trolling amid Alia Bhatt's Cannes appearance chatter Actor Sonu Sood has seemingly defended Alia Bhatt amid online chatter over her Cannes appearance. His cryptic message has sparked fresh discussion as fans and fellow actors rally behind Alia.

Beyond the tapori: Anil Kapoor's new life as Bollywood's hero in uniform Anil Kapoor moved away from his vintage tapori and romantic star days long ago. But he seems to be reinventing himself lately as a new-age hero in uniform, with projects such as 24, Subedaar, War 2 and Fighter.

Maa Behen: Madhuri Dixit, Triptii Dimri's film releases on Netflix in June Netflix has set June 4 as the premiere date for Maa Behen, starring Madhuri Dixit, Triptii Dimri, Dharna Durgaa and Ravi Kishan. The crime comedy follows a mother and her two daughters as they scramble to hide a dead body and protect their secret.

Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 streams uncut on Netflix US. Here's what is different Dhurandhar: The Revenge has begun streaming on Netflix internationally in a raw and uncut version with longer, more explicit scenes. The release has drawn attention online, with viewers comparing its restored violence and uncensored dialogue to the theatrical cut.

Rajinikanth's Jailer 2 crew member dies after electric shock on Chennai set A 28-year-old art department worker died after suffering an electric shock on the Jailer 2 set in Chennai. Police are investigating the suspected electrical leak, while the makers are yet to issue a statement.

general news.headline: Oil reserves to LPG supply: PM's 2.5-hour UAE trip brings big returns for IndiaIndia and the UAE signed key defence, energy and maritime agreements and announced USD 5 billion in investments amid regional geopolitical uncertainty.

Disputed Bhojshala site is temple of Goddess Saraswati: Madhya Pradesh High CourtThe High Court ruled Bhojshala's religious character as a Saraswati temple, ended the shared worship arrangement, directed ASI to oversee preservation, and suggested separate land for a mosque.

Virtual hearings, 2-day WFH, carpool for judges: SC joins PM's save fuel pushAfter PM Modi's appeal to conserve fuel amid the West Asia crisis, the Supreme Court introduced virtual hearings, work-from-home arrangements and car-pooling measures to improve fuel efficiency and optimise administrative functioning.

Why F-16 fighter jets escorted PM Modi's aircraft in UAEUAE F-16 fighter jets escorted Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Air India One as it entered the country's airspace on Friday. The gesture highlighted the close ties between Delhi and Abu Dhabi.

Why India sent 47,000 kg of gold to UK in 1991When India faced a balance-of-payments crisis in the late 1980s and early 1990s, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in 1991 airlifted nearly 47,000 kg of gold to England to raise a $405-million emergency loan and prevent a sovereign default. The shipment, carried out in secrecy and under tight security, was one of the defining moments that paved the way for India's economic reforms.

To turn coal into much-needed gas and fertiliser, India promises Rs 37,500 croreAs global energy shocks have exposed India's import vulnerabilities, New Delhi is betting big on coal gasification to turn its vast domestic reserves into fuels, fertilisers and chemicals. India has coal reserves that can last 200 years. The Modi government this week approved a Rs 37,500-crore scheme to promote gasification projects.

Rajasthan family at centre of NEET leak row had obtained 2025 paper as well: SourcesInvestigators found that all five children of the Biwal family allegedly received the NEET 2025 paper before the exam, while Vikas Biwal, son of alleged kingpin Mangilal, is accused of helping circulate the leaked paper after Rs 65 lakh was allegedly paid to procure it.

Unemployed youth like cockroaches, parasites: CJI slams those who attack systemDuring a Supreme Court hearing, Chief Justice Surya Kant likened some unemployed activists to "cockroaches" while rebuking a lawyer seeking Senior Advocate designation. The bench questioned the lawyer's posts, warned it could cancel any designation, and raised doubts over lawyers' degrees.

Can dogs penetrate humans? NYT op-ed on rape of Palestinians by IDF sparks outrageWhat began as a disturbing opinion piece by Nicholas Kristof in The New York Times has now spiralled into a global debate. In his piece in the NYT, Kristof alleges the use of dogs to rape Palestinian prisoners by Israeli forces. Amid the repulsion it has triggered, experts are debating if human penetration by dogs is biologically feasible.

Golden gate to Central Asia: Iran urges India not to abandon Chabahar PortIranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Chabahar Port remains a key symbol of India-Iran cooperation and urged Delhi to continue investing in it despite US sanctions.

Xi says US is declining. Trump turns it into public post, redirects it to BidenTrump framed Xi's "declining America" remark as a criticism of the Biden era, while portraying his own presidency as a period of economic revival, military strength and renewed US global dominance during the ongoing Beijing summit.

Breach in chain of command: Education Minister owns up to NEET paper leak lapseSpeaking at a press conference on May 15, the Education Minister acknowledged a "breach in the chain of command" and said the government was taking responsibility for the NEET paper leak controversy.

Pati Patni Aur Woh Do review: Funny, harmless and comfortably paisa vasoolRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026Pati Patni Aur Woh Do brings Ayushmann Khurrana back to his small-town comic comfort zone. The film stays light and crowd-pleasing and avoids saying anything deeper - a clean, breezy entertainer.

If I have to prove my worth…: Virat Kohli breaks silence on retirement talksFormer India captain Virat Kohli has addressed the growing chatter around his future and the 2027 ODI World Cup, saying he has no interest in being in an environment where he constantly has to prove his worth despite his commitment and preparation for the game.

Rain coming: Southwest monsoon to enter India, hit Kerala on May 27The forecast carries a margin of error of plus or minus four days, meaning the monsoon could arrive anywhere between May 22 and May 30.

Advisor to PM in race for Bengal Finance Minister's post, via NandigramEconomist Sanjeev Sanyal has emerged as the leading contender for Bengal's finance portfolio. The buzz has grown amid speculation that the Suvendu Adhikari-led government lacks a strong finance hand to steer Bengal's economy.

Pak's reply to Dhurandhar, Mera Lyari, sells just 22 tickets, pulled from hallsPakistan's much-hyped film Mera Lyari, sold only 22 tickets upon release and was reportedly pulled from many theatres on its first day. The film was touted as Pak's reply to India's Dhurandhar films.

Petrol, diesel prices hiked after years. Here's what else could get costlierRising petrol and diesel prices are starting to push up transport and household costs across India. Economists say the impact could spread to food, deliveries, air travel and inflation if crude stays high.

When IIMs took to academic roasting with paper-leak questions after CAT cancellationMonths after the 2003 CAT question paper's leak forced the cancellation of the premier exam, the IIMs returned with a re-test featuring questions that mirrored the mechanics of the scandal itself. The now-legendary 'mastermind' CAT question is remembered by CAT aspirants as a rare moment which many call 'academic roasting'.

Man calls wife's lover home for drinks, then slits his throat in front of herAccording to officials, the incident took place on Wednesday when the accused, identified as Bhimraj Omprakash Sharma, invited Vikas Ashok Bhusare to his home. Sharma allegedly suspected Bhusare of having an affair with his wife.

Karuppu review: A nostalgic but uneven ride with Suriya at his bestRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026Karuppu movie review: Director RJ Balaji's Karuppu, starring Suriya, Trisha and himself, is a fantasy courtroom drama that exposes how corruption is deeply ingrained in the system. While the film taps into a familiar idea, it doesn't capitalise and use it to its full potential.

Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari suspends ex-top Kolkata cop, 2 others over RG KarSuvendu Adhikari announced the suspension of three IPS officers over alleged lapses in the RG Kar case. The move follows continued questions over the investigation and the victim family's demand for accountability.

Petrol, diesel prices could rise over next 3-4 months amid crude oil crisisThe Rs 3-per-litre fuel price hike may not be the last, with analysts warning that persistently high crude oil prices could trigger more petrol and diesel price increases over coming three to four months.

Not Bengal, rasgulla is from Odisha: Chef Kunal Kapur reopens the sweet debateOdisha or West Bengal — which state does the rasgulla really belong to? While this remains one of India's most deliciously evergreen food debates, celebrity chef Kunal Kapur has now added his own sweet-and-spicy take to the conversation.

sports.headline: LSG vs CSK Live: Chennai hope to keep play-off charge alive Lucknow (LSG) vs Chennai (CSK) Live Score, IPL 2026 Today's Match Updates: Chennai Super Kings will look to strengthen their play-off push when they take on Lucknow Super Giants, who have endured a disappointing campaign and struggled for consistency throughout the season.

Eyes on 2036 Olympics: India looking at their pathways to secure hosting rights A Sports Ministry review of Commonwealth Games preparations also included a discussion on advancing India's 2036 Olympic bid. The conversation reflected planning around Ahmedabad's sporting roadmap even as the IOC process remains frozen.

New Zealand's Rachin Ravindra leaves KKR camp to prepare for England Tests Rachin Ravindra has left the Kolkata Knight Riders camp to begin preparations for New Zealand's upcoming Test tour of England. His departure comes at a critical juncture of IPL 2026, with KKR facing a string of must-win matches to keep their playoff hopes alive in an increasingly tight race.

China secures broadcasting deal for FIFA World Cup 2026. Will India follow suit? China has secured a FIFA World Cup broadcast deal, but India is still without a rights holder. The delay has now reached the Delhi High Court as questions grow over public access before kickoff.

Virat Kohli explains why helping IPL's new stars like Prince Yadav is worth it Virat Kohli said he is happy to advise young stars like Prince Yadav during the IPL, even if it comes at the cost of his own wicket during matches. Kohli revealed how he told Prince Yadav on how to dismiss him.

ICC penalises Pakistan with WTC point deduction and fine after Dhaka defeat The ICC fined Pakistan 40 percent of their match fees and docked eight World Test Championship points for a slow over-rate during the first Test against Bangladesh in Dhaka.

What've you done for Punjab? Arshdeep lands in hot water again after Snapchat rant Punjab Kings pacer Arshdeep Singh came under fresh scrutiny after a Snapchat reply to a fan went viral amid an ongoing controversy surrounding his remarks in IPL-related videos.

CSK, RCB sign replacements for Khaleel Ahmed and Nuwan Thushara CSK signed domestic pacer Kuldip Yadav for Rs 30 lakh after Khaleel Ahmed suffered a quadriceps tear. Meanwhile, RCB signed Richard Gleeson for Rs 1.6 crore following Nuwan Thushara's withdrawal.

Are PBKS being affected by the off-field drama? Haddin reacts after MI loss Brad Haddin has dismissed suggestions that the Punjab Kings are being rattled by off-field controversy after their defeat to Mumbai Indians. He said the real issue is Punjab's failure under pressure, with two must-win matches left in the playoff race.

If I have to prove my worth…: Virat Kohli breaks silence on retirement talks Former India captain Virat Kohli has addressed the growing chatter around his future and the 2027 ODI World Cup, saying he has no interest in being in an environment where he constantly has to prove his worth despite his commitment and preparation for the game.

Were you approached for fixing? Sourav Ganguly recalls asking Sachin, Dravid Sourav Ganguly said he once asked Sachin Tendulkar, Rahul Dravid and Anil Kumble whether they had ever been approached for match-fixing, recalling the uncertainty and distrust that surrounded Indian cricket during the scandal-hit period of 2000.

LSG vs CSK: Chennai eye playoff push as Lucknow look to spoil the partyIPL 2026, LSG vs CSK: Chennai Super Kings will look to keep their IPL 2026 playoff hopes alive when they take on Lucknow Super Giants, who will aim to play spoilsport after being knocked out of the tournament.

Did MI hit back at Arshdeep's 'andhera' jibe on Tilak? Latest post sparks buzzMumbai Indians' celebratory post after Tilak Varma's match-winning knock has reignited scrutiny of Arshdeep Singh's 'andhera' remark. Arshdeep landed himself in hot water after his Snapchat video went viral for the wrong reasons.

Samson next India T20I captain? Shastri gives his seal of approval for CSK starRavi Shastri has backed Sanju Samson as a serious candidate to lead India in T20Is. Shastri feels that Samson has answered his critics by showing consistency during the T20 World Cup and the ongoing IPL season.

I only listen to Rohit Sharma: Tilak Varma's heartfelt tribute after win vs PBKSMumbai Indians star Tilak Varma paid an emotional on-field tribute to Rohit Sharma after defeating Punjab Kings. The youngster dedicated the win to the veteran opener, gesturing his jersey number 45 in Dharamsala.

MI batting coach Pollard cops hefty fine after outburst at umpire in PBKS clashPBKS vs MI: Mumbai Indians batting coach Kieron Pollard has been fined 15 per cent of his match fee for using abusive language towards the fourth umpire. The penalty came during a tense six-wicket victory over Punjab Kings in Dharamsala.

Thailand Open 2026 live: Lakshya fights back Vitidsarn in 1st gameThailand Open 2026 Badminton Live Updates: Lakshya Sen is in action soon with Kunlavut Vitidsarn in the men's singles quarterfinal clash on Court 1. The Thai opponent has taken a big start against Lakshya, and the India star is chasing Game 1.

Why has BCCI picked Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for India A and not India15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has earned his maiden India A call-up for the tour of Sri Lanka in June following his sensational performances for Bihar, India U-19 and the Rajasthan Royals in the Indian Premier League.

Mumbai Indians players are happier when Hardik is not there: Kris SrikkanthKris Srikkanth said the Mumbai Indians looked lighter and more united without Hardik Pandya in the dugout after the win over Punjab Kings. MI pulled off a stunning performance to defeat PBKS in Dharamsala.

Ban Arshdeep: L Sivaramakrishnan urges BCCI action for Tilak Varma racist remarkLaxman Sivaramakrishnan has called for a season-long ban for Arshdeep Singh over a "racist" remark toward Tilak Varma. The veteran claims the BCCI has enough proof to act, stating that the modern game is a profession, not just a game.

Mbappe captain, Camavinga out as France announce FIFA World Cup 2026 squadDidier Deschamps has named France's 2026 World Cup squad with Kylian Mbappe as captain and Eduardo Camavinga omitted. It sharpens the focus on France's depth ahead of what is expected to be Deschamps' final World Cup in charge.

Sreejesh was insulted: Former captain Jude Felix tears into Hockey IndiaJude Felix has accused Hockey India of insulting PR Sreejesh by removing him from the junior men's team job. The former India captain also questioned the federation's decision to appoint Sreejesh and then not give him the required time in the role.

IPL Play of the Day: Captain Jasprit Bumrah, the friend at mid-offIPL 2026, PBKS vs MI: In an era built to break bowlers, Jasprit Bumrah strapped on the captain's armband for the first time in his IPL career and did something no stat line could capture: he became his bowlers' best friend.

Flat decks gone, Tilak Varma shows his class in DharamsalaNamed the captain of India A for the tri-nation series, Tilak Varma proved the selectors right with a terrific knock against Punjab Kings. With pitches now slowing down, Tilak is showing that he is one of the class acts in the IPL.

Shardul Thakur addresses Hardik Pandya's prolonged absence amid trade rumoursIPL 2026: Mumbai Indians seamer Shardul Thakur has addressed captain Hardik Pandya's prolonged absence from the side. Shardul responded to the trade rumours that have caught fire on social media.

Tilak Varma scales new heights as Punjab's freefall in the mountains continuesIPL 2026, PBKS vs MI: Tilak Varma's blazing 75 lit up Dharamsala. Jasprit Bumrah marshals his troops with quiet authority on captaincy debut, and Punjab Kings find the mountain air thinner and colder with every passing defeat.

Preity Zinta, Ricky Ponting in tense chat after Punjab Kings crash in DharamsalaPunjab Kings co-owner Preity Zinta had a concerned look after the side lost their 5th game in a row in IPL 2026. In a nail-bitter, Mumbai Indians beat Shreyas Iyer's side by 6 wickets in a final-over thriller in Dharamsala.

Shoddy PBKS lose 5 in a row. Can Shreyas Iyer's men qualify for playoffs?IPL 2026, PBKS vs MI: Punjab Kings have now lost five matches in a row and find themselves in a tricky position heading into their final two league games, with the race for the playoffs intensifying with every passing match.

Nikhat Zareen hits out at BFI-SAI tiff after crashing out of Asiad raceNikhat Zareen has hit out at the Boxing Federation of India and the Sports Authority of India after crashing out of the Asian Games and Commonwealth Games selection race. Nikhat said the last two weeks had been "torturous" for Indian boxers amid the ongoing tussle between the two bodies.

PR Sreejesh raises tough questions for Hockey India after controversial coaching exitPR Sreejesh has questioned Hockey India's coaching structure and growing reliance on foreign staff after his controversial exit from the junior men's setup despite a successful 18-month tenure.

Top government officers, bureaucrats enjoy sports fund meant for athletesAn investigation by The Indian Express has alleged that crores from the National Sports Development Fund were spent on sports infrastructure linked to bureaucrats and exclusive government institutions instead of athletes and public sporting projects.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
